classObjective: Interpret orthographic drawings to build models of three-dimensional figures.
workedExamples: 11-6, Example 1 — Build a Model from an Orthographic Drawing
11-6, Example 2 — Make an Orthographic Drawing from a Model
warmUp: What three views are typically shown in an orthographic drawing?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: top, front, left/right views from orthographic drawings; building 3D models from 2D representations
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice interpreting orthographic drawings and building corresponding 3D models
independentPractice: Practice problems creating models from orthographic drawings and drawings from models
assessment: Exit ticket: given an orthographic drawing (top, front, right views), sketch the 3D figure it represents
capReflection: Why is orthographic drawing important in engineering and architecture?
